channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Florida raccoon crowned 2024 Cadbury Bunny
item.description: HERSHEY, Pa. (WCMH) — A new Cadbury Bunny has been named in the sixth annual Cadbury Bunny Tryouts. The 2024 crown went to Louie, the first-ever raccoon to win the contest, according to a news release from Hershey.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Thompson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US pastors see popular Easter services as opportunity to rebuild in-person worship attendance
item.description: As pastors prepare for Easter this year, many are eager to welcome more people back to churches than they have seen since the onset of the COVID-19 pandemic four years ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: William, Kate reject Meghan, Harry visit after cancer diagnosis: Sources
item.description: William and Kate are said to have washed their hands of Harry and Meghan and are “not interested in any drama” that a visit might bring.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Trump will attend the wake of a slain New York police officer as he goes after Biden over crime
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Donald Trump will attend Thursday's wake of a New York City police officer gunned down in the line of duty, as the presumptive Republican presidential nominee has made crime a focus of his third White House campaign and accused President Joe Biden of lacking toughness.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Putin says he won't start a war with NATO. But Western bases hosting Ukraine F-16s would be targets
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin scoffed at the possibility of his country launching an attack on a NATO member, calling it “sheer nonsense,” but warned that any Western air base hosting U.S.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and BARRY HATTON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Fallen crypto mogul Sam Bankman-Fried sentenced to 25 years
item.description: Bankman-Fried, 32, was convicted in November of fraud and conspiracy — a dramatic fall from a crest of success that included a Super Bowl advertisement and celebrity endorsements.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Missing Tennessee teen Sebastian Rogers' mother defends leaving neighborhood after ‘threatening’ messages
item.description: Tennessee 15-year-old Sebastian Rogers has been missing since Feb. 26, when he left home without shoes. He was last seen in the area of Stafford Court in Hendersonville.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Farmer explains new Chick-fil-A chicken policy
item.description: Chick-fil-A executives notified customers of the switch through the restaurant’s app with a page called "Our Chicken Commitment.
item.dc:creator: Shaquira Speaks, NewsNation

item.title: What's keeping egg prices high for Easter? It's not just inflation
item.description: Egg prices are at near-historic highs in many parts of the world as the spring holidays approach, reflecting a market scrambled by disease, high demand and growing costs for farmers.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Jonathan Diller shooting: NYPD sergeants' union tells anti-police Democrats to stay away from funeral
item.description: Police union slams progressive Democrats blamed for anti-cop policies, warning city leaders to stay away from slain NYPD Officer Jonathan Diller's funeral.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What we know about the Baltimore bridge collapse
item.description: A cargo ship rammed into Baltimore’s Francis Scott Bridge, causing the span to collapse and presumably killing six construction workers.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and JAMES POLLARD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal EV charging stations are key to Biden's climate agenda, yet only 4 states have them
item.description: Electric vehicle charging ports are a key part of President Joe Biden’s effort to encourage drivers to move away from gasoline-powered cars and trucks that contribute to global warming, but only four states have them.
item.dc:creator: Alexa St. John, Matthew Daly And Joshua A. Bickel, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Barges are bringing cranes to Baltimore to help remove bridge wreckage and open shipping route
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Massive barges carrying cranes streamed toward Baltimore on Thursday to begin the challenging work of removing twisted metal and concrete in an attempt to open a key shipping route blocked by the wreckage of the Francis Scott Key Bridge. U.S.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE and BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Amid wars and White House race, celebs weigh risks of speaking out
item.description: Celebrities went all in during the 2020 and 2016 White House races, but they are proceeding with more caution this election cycle.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KRON4

item.title: West Point cadet, 21, found dead in Florida during spring break trip
item.description: A West Point cadet was found dead this week following what officials believe to be an accidental drowning, the United States Military Academy has confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KTLA 5

item.title: US Census changes how it categorizes people by race and ethnicity
item.description: Story at a glance The 2030 U.S. Census will include new race and ethnicity checkboxes for Hispanic people and people of Middle Eastern and North African descent, the Office of Management and Budget announced Thursday, a major change in how the government tracks demographics.
item.dc:creator: Nick Robertson, The Hill

item.title: Universal's 'How To Train Your Dragon' land revealed for Epic Universe
item.description: How to Train Your Dragon – Isle of Berk will be one of five worlds that will be featured in the all-new Universal Epic Universe set to open in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, KRON4

item.title: Don't touch! These fuzzy bugs are taking over Florida
item.description: Springtime ushers in an influx of an infamous Florida pest. They appear to be everywhere, but be careful not to touch them.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, The Hill

item.title: Schools in the path of April's total solar eclipse prepare for a natural teaching moment
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Seventh-grade student Henry Cohen bounced side to side in time to the Beatles’ “Here Comes the Sun” playing in teacher Nancy Morris’ classroom, swinging his arms open and closed across the planets pictured on his T-shirt.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: VP Harris says US agencies must show their AI tools aren't harming people's safety or rights
item.description: U.S. federal agencies must show that their artificial intelligence tools aren't harming the public, or stop using them, under new rules unveiled by the White House on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: South Texas lawmaker asks governor declare emergency due to 'water crisis'
item.description: A South Texas lawmaker on Wednesday asked Gov. Greg Abbott to declare an emergency due to what he calls a water "crisis" on the border in the Rio Grande Valley.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: King Charles III in Easter message calls for kindness after cancer diagnosis
item.description: King Charles III shared a message Thursday calling for kindness and friendship “in a time of need,” after he and Kate, the Princess of Wales, were both diagnosed with cancer.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: US changes how it categorizes people by race and ethnicity. It's the first revision in 27 years
item.description: For the first time in 27 years, the U.S. government is changing how it categorizes people by race and ethnicity.
item.dc:creator: Mike Schneider, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Venezuelans are increasingly stuck in Mexico, explaining drop in illegal crossings to US
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Venezuelan migrants often have a quick answer when asked to name the most difficult stretch of their eight-country journey to the U.S. border, and it's not the dayslong jungle trek through Colombia and Panama with its venomous vipers, giant spiders and scorpions. It's Mexico.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER SHERMAN, ELLIOT SPAGAT and VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden is announcing a new rule to protect consumers who purchase short-term health insurance plans
item.description: President Joe Biden on Thursday announced new steps to protect consumers who buy short-term health insurance plans that critics say amount to junk. A new rule finalized by the Democratic president's administration will limit these plans to just three months.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Still climbing: Powerball jackpot increases to $935 million
item.description: (AP) — The Powerball jackpot increased to an estimated $935 million after no one matched the six numbers drawn Wednesday night. The numbers selected were: 37, 46, 57, 60, 66 and the Powerball 8.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Nobelist Daniel Kahneman, a pioneer of behavioral economics, is dead at 90
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Daniel Kahneman, a psychologist who won a Nobel Prize in economics for his insights into how ingrained neurological biases influence decision making, died Wednesday at the age of 90.
item.dc:creator: DAVID HAMILTON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hidden camera found in staff bathroom at children's hospital in Rhode Island: police
item.description: The employee accused of placing the camera in the bathroom has since been fired, according to a hospital spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Doiron, Fox 5

item.title: Army Corps of Engineers to deploy 1,100 personnel to Baltimore after bridge collapse
item.description: The crumbling bridge sent multiple cars and construction workers who had been filling potholes plunging into the chilly waters below.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: TIMELINE: NTSB details moments before cargo ship crashed into Baltimore bridge
item.description: The NTSB received six hours of voyage data recorder (VDR) data spanning from midnight to 6 a.m. on the day of the crash.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, Fox 5

item.title: 4 dead and 7 injured in Illinois stabbings; suspect in custody: police
item.description: Police in Illinois said 4 people, including a mail carrier, are dead and 7 injured after a series of attacks by a suspect in a southeast Rockford neighborhood Wednesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: John Clark, Fox 5

item.title: Most Americans support cannabis legalization for medical, recreational use: Pew
item.description: Story at a glance Most Americans — 88 percent — think cannabis should be legal for medical or recreational use, according to a recently published Pew Research Center survey.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, The Hill

item.title: Disney, DeSantis-backed board reach settlement agreement
item.description: Disney and the state of Florida have reached a settlement agreement in their lawsuit about who controls Walt Disney World’s governing district.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9

item.title: Activists form human chain across Nashville, Tennessee, on Covenant school shooting anniversary
item.description: Tennessee advocates for gun safety formed a three-mile human chain in Nashville on the anniversary of last year's deadly Covenant School shooting.
item.dc:creator: Travis Loller And Jonathan Mattise, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Recovery efforts resume for 6 presumed dead in Baltimore bridge collapse
item.description: BALTIMORE — Recovery efforts continued in Baltimore early Wednesday morning for six construction workers who were on the Francis Scott Key Bridge when it was crashed into by a cargo ship and collapsed early Tuesday morning. All six are missing and presumed dead.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Hardy souls across New England and New York dig out after major winter storm
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Thousands of hardy souls across New England spent Sunday digging out after a major weekend storm dumped more than two feet of snow in some areas, caused multiple road accidents, downed power lines and left hundreds of thousands across the Northeast in the dark, some perhaps for days.
item.dc:creator: STEVE LeBLANC, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 85 years after a racist mob drove Opal Lee's family away, she's getting a new home on the same spot
item.description: FORT WORTH, Texas (AP) — When Opal Lee was 12, a racist mob drove her family out of their Texas home. Now, the 97-year-old community activist is getting closer to moving into a brand new home on the very same tree-lined corner lot in Fort Worth.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, WGN 9
